fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Texas Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity texas, how to transfer physical therapist license to texas, physical therapist compact texas, texas physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Texas Executive Council of PT and OT Examiners (ECPTOTE)
board.parent_dept: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Texas.

steps.title: Complete Education Requirements
steps.description: Complete a CAPTE-accredited Doctor of Physical Therapy (DPT) program and any required clinical practicum or fieldwork.

steps.title: Pass the NPTE
steps.description: Successfully pass the required NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

steps.title: Submit Application and Fees
steps.description: Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary licensing fees.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Texas physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Texas have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Texas participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Texas without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Texas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.
